channel.title: Skills Workflow LinkedIn Posts
channel.description: Latest public LinkedIn company posts from Skills Workflow, exposed as RSS.

item.title: Your consultants are billing about 69% of available hours.
item.description:
Your consultants are billing about 69% of available hours. The other 31% — most firms genuinely can't tell you where it went.
At a 25-person firm billing €700/day, that's roughly €52,000 a month in unbilled capacity.
Most of those hours weren't lost to laziness. They were lost to miscommunication, skills mismatches, and project managers defaulting to whoever they already knew was good — regardless of who actually had capacity.
What I keep seeing at consulting firms is this: a PM needs someone with, say, financial modeling and change management experience. There's no system that surfaces that combination. So they email a few people, book whoever responds first, and end up with the same senior consultant at 130% utilisation — while two people with the right skills and open schedules don't get the call.
Six weeks later: two people burnt out, one underutilised, the project margin squeezed.
In Skills Workflow, resource assignment works off skill tags, client history, and real-time availability. The PM can see — before they start calling around — who has the right profile AND capacity for this specific engagement.
Challenge: Pull your utilisation report for last quarter and try to explain, by name and project, where the under-billed hours went. Most firms I've worked with can't. The hours weren't gone — they were just never visible enough to protect.
#AgencyOps #SkillsWorkflow #ConsultingFirm #ResourceManagement #AgencyManagement

item.title: Most agencies don’t run a workflow.
item.description:
Most agencies don’t run a workflow.
They run a relay race.

Brief in one tool.
Projects in another.
Time in spreadsheets.
Billing elsewhere.

𝐒𝐢𝐱 𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐠𝐞𝐬 𝐥𝐨𝐬𝐞 𝐭𝐢𝐦𝐞 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐦𝐚𝐫𝐠𝐢𝐧

1. Brief intake
2. Scope and estimation
3. Resource allocation
4. Production and approvals
5. Time tracking and delivery
6. Billing and reporting

𝐖𝐡𝐞𝐧 𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐬𝐢𝐱 𝐜𝐨𝐧𝐧𝐞𝐜𝐭
→ handoff gaps reduce
→ data re entry drops
→ invoicing becomes predictable

Which stage is your bottleneck
𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝟏 𝐭𝐨 𝟔
Or
𝐃𝐌 𝐁𝟐𝐁 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐚 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐭

item.title: Most creative agencies manage their resources with a WhatsApp group and a shared Google...
item.description:
Most creative agencies manage their resources with a WhatsApp group and a shared Google Calendar. True story!

A producer pings the group asking who's free next week. The account director checks the calendar to see if anyone's double-booked. The ops manager keeps a spreadsheet that's three days out of date.
And somehow, a project gets staffed.
It works. Until it doesn't.
A designer gets pulled onto two campaigns at once. A developer goes from zero to fully booked with nothing in between. Three weeks later, you find out your best copywriter is sitting at 140% capacity while two junior creatives have had barely a day of work between them.
At that point, you've got a visibility problem, not a headcount problem.

The agencies I've seen grow well aren't always the ones with the best talent. They're the ones who know exactly where that talent is at any given moment.

In Skills Workflow, resource assignment happens by skills, availability, and client affinity, not by whoever responds to a message first. Managers see workload views across the full team. Capacity gaps surface before they become crises.

Last year, 59% of agencies grew revenue. Only 31% improved their margins. A lot of that gap lives in the invisible stuff, the overbooked designers, the idle juniors, the projects you said no to because you didn't think you had capacity.
You had capacity. You just couldn't see it.

What tool does your agency actually use for resource planning right now?

#AgencyOps #ResourceManagement #CreativeAgency #SkillsWorkflow #AgencyManagement

…more

item.title: Enterprise agencies have hundreds of people.
item.description:
Enterprise agencies have hundreds of people.
Boutiques have a handful.

Mid-market agencies fall in between.

Not enough bodies to overwhelm problems.
Not small enough to wing it.

The agencies winning in the middle
don't have more resources.

They allocate smarter.

Skills Workflow's Automatic Resource Prioritization
makes every person count.

The system analyzes:
→ Team skills and experience levels
→ Current workload and availability
→ Project priority and client value
→ Deadline urgency and dependencies

Then suggests optimal assignments.

Not who asked first.
Who's actually best positioned to deliver.

Agencies report significant utilization improvements.

Same team size.
More billable work.
Zero new hires.

They stopped playing resource Tetris
and started using data.

Senior talent works on high-value projects.
Junior talent gets appropriate assignments.
Nobody gets double-booked.
Capacity is visible in real-time.

Swipe through to see how smart allocation works →

Book a demo: https://lnkd.in/gTRTFd_V

Make your mid-sized team perform like a larger one.

item.title: Most PMs are making budget decisions based on week-old data.
item.description:
Most PMs are making budget decisions
based on week-old data.

By the time they see the report, they're already over.

Real-time budget tracking changes everything.

Client asks: "How much budget do we have left?"

You should know the answer instantly.

Instead, you're calculating:
→ Time logged so far
→ Expenses submitted
→ Outstanding vendor invoices
→ Team hours scheduled ahead
→ Resources already allocated

By the time you compile everything,
the number is already outdated.

Here's the problem:

Budget tracking happens after the spending.
Not during the spending.

Finance runs reports weekly.
But projects burn budget daily.

Skills Workflow tracks budget burn in real-time.

Every hour logged → updates budget automatically
Every expense approved → deducted instantly
Every resource assigned → calculated against remaining budget

Project at 60% complete?
See exactly how much budget remains.
Right now. Not next week.

PMs make better decisions
because they see financial impact immediately.

"Should we add another designer to this?"
The system shows remaining budget.
Decision made in seconds.

Agencies report catching budget issues
while there's still time to adjust.

Not when the project is already over budget.
When it starts trending that way.

Swipe through to see how budget burn tracking works →

Book a demo: https://lnkd.in/gTRTFd_V

Stop discovering overruns after they happen.

item.title: You scaled the team.
item.description:
You scaled the team.
You landed bigger clients.
But ops still run like “15 people.”

𝟓 𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧𝐬 𝐲𝐨𝐮 𝐨𝐮𝐭𝐠𝐫𝐞𝐰 𝐰𝐨𝐫𝐤𝐟𝐥𝐨𝐰
→ Time tracked in spreadsheets
→ Reporting takes days
→ Briefs get lost in email
→ Unable to say profitability
→ Onboarding takes 2+ weeks

If you’re seeing 2+,
your workflow is the bottleneck.

DM me 𝐖𝐎𝐑𝐊𝐅𝐋𝐎𝐖
and 𝐈’𝐥𝐥 𝐫𝐞𝐩𝐥𝐲 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡
𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐟𝐢𝐫𝐬𝐭 𝐟𝐢𝐱 𝐭𝐨 𝐩𝐫𝐢𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐳𝐞.

#agencyoperations #creativeproduction
#operations #transformation #projectmanagement

item.title: Your first 3 hours today?
item.description:
Your first 3 hours today?
All meetings. Zero progress.

Monday morning.

First meeting: "Align on project priorities"
Second meeting: "Follow-up on client proposal status"
Third meeting: "Quick sync on team capacity"

By lunch, nothing has actually moved forward.

This is what happens when your team
can't see project status without asking.

Every status meeting exists
because someone lacks visibility.

Every coordination call exists
because information lives in someone's head.

Every "quick sync" exists
because the system doesn't show real-time updates.

Remove the meetings.
Give everyone the same visibility.

Skills Workflow shows your team:
→ Project status in real-time
→ Who's working on what
→ Whether you're on track or drifting
→ What needs attention right now

No meeting required.

Agencies report cutting status meetings
significantly after giving teams
real-time project visibility.

Same work. Fewer meetings. More progress.

Book a demo: https://lnkd.in/gTRTFd_V

Stop meeting about work.
Start doing the work.

item.title: Most project management tools force agencies into their workflow.
item.description:
Most project management tools
force agencies into their workflow.

Monday .com templates.
Asana boards.
ClickUp structures.

But your agency isn't like everyone else.

Your creative approval process has multiple stages.
Theirs has a few.

Your client reviews need legal sign-off.
Theirs don't.

Your production involves external vendors.
Theirs is all in-house.

Generic templates break when workflows get complex.

Skills Workflow's Production Process Management
adapts to how your agency actually works.

Custom approval chains.
Industry-specific workflows.
Multi-stakeholder reviews.
Vendor coordination built in.

No coding required.
No forcing your team into someone else's process.

Agencies move from multiple different tools
trying to patch together their workflow.

Now everything runs in one system
configured exactly how they need it.

Their workflow. Their rules.
Finally, software that works for them.

Swipe through to see how it works →

Book a demo: https://lnkd.in/gTRTFd_V

Stop adapting to your software.
Make it adapt to you.

item.title: Your team says they're slammed.
item.description:
Your team says they're slammed.

You check the dashboard.
Utilization: 58%

What's happening?

The disconnect is real.
And it's not because anyone is lying.

Here's what causes the gap:

Designer spends 2 hours in status meetings.
Another hour hunting down feedback from emails.
30 minutes waiting for approvals.
45 minutes context switching between 6 projects.

Actual design work? 3.5 hours.

But they worked a full 8-hour day.
Feels slammed. Looks underutilized.

The problem isn't effort.
It's operational friction eating capacity.

When you can't see the difference between:
→ Billable client work
→ Internal coordination overhead
→ Waiting on dependencies
→ Context switching tax

You can't fix what's killing productivity.

Skills Workflow shows you where time actually goes.

Not just "Sarah worked 8 hours."
But broken down into:
Billable client work
Meetings
Waiting on approvals
Admin

Now you see the real problem.
It's not utilization. It's friction.

Fix the friction.
Utilization improves automatically.

Book a demo: https://lnkd.in/gTRTFd_V

See where your team's time actually goes.

item.title: Creative work is ready.
item.description:
Creative work is ready.
Client approval takes days.

The bottleneck isn't the work.
It's the approval process.

Here's what slows everything down:

Designer uploads final files to Share Drive.
PM downloads, uploads to email.
Client downloads, reviews, sends feedback via email.
PM copies feedback into a workflow system or excel.
Designer makes revisions, uploads to Drive.
PM downloads again, sends to client again.
Client approves (finally).
PM updates project status.
PM notifies finance to bill.

Multiple steps. Multiple tools. Days wasted.

Skills Workflow's Client Collaboration Portal
cuts approval cycles dramatically.

Clients review and approve work directly in the platform.
Instant notifications. Version control. Clear feedback loops.
Automatic project status updates.
Approved deliverables queue for billing automatically.

No email chains.
No version confusion.
No wondering if the client saw it.

Agencies consistently report faster approval times.
Same clients. Same projects.
Just eliminated the friction.

Swipe through to see how it works →

Book a demo: https://lnkd.in/gTRTFd_V

Turn approval bottlenecks into your competitive advantage.

item.title: Your team has 47 active projects.
item.description:
Your team has 47 active projects.

The client portal shows:
→ 23 marked "HIGH PRIORITY"
→ 18 marked "URGENT"
→ 6 marked "CRITICAL"

When everything is urgent, nothing is.

Here's what happens:

Account director tells the team:
"This client project is top priority."

Two hours later, another account director:
"Actually, this other client needs it first."

PM tries to allocate resources.
Every project owner says theirs is most important.

Designer gets pulled in 4 directions.
Can't focus. Context switching kills productivity.

Projects that should take 3 days take 2 weeks.
Because nobody knows what to work on first.

The problem isn't your team.
It's missing objective prioritization criteria.

Skills Workflow helps agencies prioritize intelligently:

→ Project value and profitability scoring
→ Deadline urgency with real timeline impact
→ Client relationship importance
→ Resource availability and capacity
→ Dependencies between projects

The system analyzes all factors.
Recommends what actually needs attention now.

Not based on who yelled loudest.
Based on business impact.

Teams know what to work on.
Managers allocate resources confidently.
Clients get realistic timelines.

Stop letting urgency be decided by volume.
Start prioritizing by actual impact.

Book a demo: https://lnkd.in/gTRTFd_V

When priorities are clear, execution is fast.

item.title: Billable hours disappear.
item.description:
Billable hours disappear.

Not because teams aren't working.
Because time trackers live somewhere else.

Here's what happens:

A designer finishes a client call.
Opens Figma to start work.
Forgets to log time in Time Tracking System.
Remembers at 4 PM.
Can't recall exact start time.
Guesses. Underestimates.

Multiply that across your entire team.
Multiple projects per person.
Every single day.

Agencies lose significant revenue annually.
In work they can't bill.
Because they already delivered it.

The fix isn't nagging teams harder.
It's capturing time where work happens.

Skills Workflow integrates time tracking
directly into project workflows.

→ Auto-capture time when you start & end task status
→ Auto-populate from calendar blocks
→ Log time from mobile between meetings
→ Track against budgets in real-time
→ Flow straight into invoices
→ Zero separate apps to remember

Agencies recover thousands of dollars in unbilled hours
within their first month.

Just by making time tracking
part of the work.
Not homework after the work.

Teams bill more.
Agencies collect more.
Nobody opens another tab.

Book a demo: https://lnkd.in/gTRTFd_V

Stop losing billable hours to broken systems.
